description_long: This bird has a streamlined body with a dark brown head and back, accented by white and orange patches on its wings and underbelly. The plumage is mostly dark brown with scattered white spots and a prominent orange-brown area on the flanks. Its beak is slender and pointed, suitable for insectivorous or omnivorous feeding. The tail is long and appears slightly rounded at the tip, with subtle feather layering visible from above. The legs are short and appear to be a muted brown or gray color. The overall appearance suggests a medium-sized passerine bird commonly found in urban parks and gardens.
description: A medium-sized bird with dark brown plumage, white spots, and orange flanks, featuring a slender pointed beak and a slightly rounded tail.
description_view_0: Side view showing the bird's dark brown back, white spots, and orange-brown flanks.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the bird's head, beak, and the symmetry of its wings and tail.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the orange and white markings on the underbelly and flanks.
description_view_3: Top-down view revealing the feather arrangement and the rounded shape of the tail.
attribute_plumage_pattern: Mottled with white spots and orange flanks on a dark brown background
